mnemonics: BILDF (Boolean, Integer, Float, Double - common primitive types)
keyPoints: Primitive data structures are fundamental types directly operated upon by machine instructions (e.g., int, float).
Non-primitive structures are built from primitive types and include arrays, lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs.
The key classification difference: primitive types store single values, while non-primitive types organize collections of data.
commonMistakes: Confusing 'primitive' with 'simple' - arrays are simple but are non-primitive as they are derived.
Assuming all basic types (like strings) are primitive - in some languages, strings are non-primitive objects.
Misclassifying structures like stacks or queues as primitive - they are abstract data types built from non-primitive structures.
